prompt:
Streaming platform team. Every release train we promote event schemas to the shared Schema Registry by hand with curl, and last month someone force-registered an incompatible orders schema and broke three consumers. I want a proper release-gate client in this repo. It talks to the registry's REST API directly - we deliberately do NOT pull Confluent jars into the CI image.

Do the homework first: before changing code, use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Confluent Schema Registry docs (docs.confluent.io - the API reference and the usage examples). Confirm the exact media type, how POST /subjects/{subject}/versions and POST /subjects/{subject} (lookup) differ, how a compatibility check against 'latest' works with verbose output, the schema references array shape, when schemaType must be sent, and what the official docs say about 429 handling for registry clients. Our pinned contract is docs/contract.json (provenance in docs/official_sources.json) - both protected. If current official docs contradict the fixture, flag the conflict in your final summary and leave the fixtures/tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final response.

What TestMain.java expects (it's the acceptance harness - loopback fake registry, dummy creds; run: java TestMain.java):

- SchemaReference with public final name/subject/version; SchemaRegistryException (checked) with public final httpStatus/errorCode; RegisteredSchema {id}; LookupResult {subject, id, version, schema}; CompatibilityResult {isCompatible, messages}; PromotionResult {outcome, id, version, messages}.
- SchemaRegistryClient(baseUrl, username, password, java.util.function.IntConsumer sleeper): register / lookup (null when the schema or subject isn't there) / checkCompatibility(subject, "latest", ...) with verbose messages. Every call sends the registry's versioned media type and Basic auth. On 429: call the sleeper with the Retry-After seconds (default it sensibly when the header's missing - the contract fixture pins the value), retry the SAME request, bounded retries, then surface the 429. Never Thread.sleep.
- ReleaseGate(client).promote(subject, schemaType, schema, references) -> PromotionResult. Discipline, in order: if the exact schema is already registered under the subject -> ALREADY_REGISTERED (no compat call, no register). Otherwise gate on compatibility vs latest -> INCOMPATIBLE with the verbose reasons, and never register. Otherwise register, then resolve the version the registry assigned -> REGISTERED. A brand-new subject (40401 on the compat check) is a first registration, not an error. A 429 storm must never end with the same schema registered twice - the harness counts writes.
- Body discipline per the docs: schemaType omitted for AVRO, sent for JSON/PROTOBUF; references omitted when empty, otherwise exactly {name, subject, version} per entry.

Split the code sensibly (client / gate / small types; there's a starter Json.java you can use or replace). TestMain.java and docs/ are protected - don't modify them.

research.interface: Schema Registry REST API, media type application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json (Confluent Platform, current docs)

official_sources.title: Schema Registry API Reference for Confluent Platform
official_sources.used_for: media types (application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json preferred, +json/application/json fallbacks); POST /subjects/{subject}/versions request fields schema/schemaType/references with reference objects of {name, subject, version}; POST /subjects/{subject} lookup request/response (subject, id, version, schema) and 40403; POST /compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version} with 'latest', verbose=true and is_compatible/messages; error codes 40401, 40402, 42201, 42202

official_sources.title: Schema Registry API Usage Examples for Confluent Platform
official_sources.used_for: exact register/lookup/compatibility request examples with Content-Type application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json; register response carrying the schema id; lookup response {subject, version, id, schema}; {"is_compatible": true} compatibility response; the rule that the response includes schemaType for JSON Schema and Protobuf but omits it for the default AVRO

official_sources.title: Release Notes for Confluent Platform 7.7
official_sources.used_for: documented 429 rate-limit behavior: 'Schema Registry clients built with Confluent Platform 7.7.4 and later include auto retries for 429 exceptions' — the basis for this client's bounded 429 retry policy

research.language_rationale: Confluent's official Schema Registry client is the Java library (io.confluent:kafka-schema-registry-client), so Java is the native language for registry tooling; this release gate speaks the documented REST surface directly with java.net.http so the CI image needs no Confluent packages, while staying byte-compatible with the documented v1 media type contract.
